name: azure-eventhub-ts
description: Build event streaming applications using Azure Event Hubs SDK for JavaScript (@azure/event-hubs). Use when implementing high-throughput event ingestion, real-time analytics, IoT telemetry, or event-driven architectures with partitioned consumers.
capabilities: Create EventHubProducerClient and EventHubConsumerClient with DefaultAzureCredential authentication
Send events with batch operations and partition targeting
Receive events with consumer groups and checkpointing using Azure Blob Storage
Configure partition-specific event consumption with offset and time-based positions
Handle transient and fatal errors with appropriate retry and recovery strategies
Access Event Hub metadata including partition properties and sequence numbers
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains documentation for the official Azure Event Hubs SDK for TypeScript. Static analyzer flagged Markdown code block delimiters as shell commands (false positive). All code patterns are standard Azure SDK usage: environment variable configuration, DefaultAzureCredential authentication, and Azure Storage endpoints for checkpointing. No security concerns identified.
valueStatement: Implement high-throughput event ingestion and real-time data processing with Azure Event Hubs. This skill provides TypeScript SDK patterns for producers, consumers, and checkpointing strategies.
userTitle: Build Azure Event Hubs streaming applications
limitations: Requires existing Azure Event Hubs namespace and event hub resources
Does not provision or manage Azure infrastructure
Supports JavaScript and TypeScript implementations only
Requires proper Azure IAM configuration for authentication

useCases.title: IoT Telemetry Ingestion
useCases.description: Stream sensor data from thousands of IoT devices to Azure Event Hubs for real-time processing and analytics.

useCases.title: Real-Time Analytics Pipeline
useCases.description: Build event-driven data pipelines that process streaming data with checkpointing for exactly-once semantics.

useCases.title: Event-Driven Microservices
useCases.description: Implement publish-subscribe patterns for decoupled microservices communication using consumer groups.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Event Producer Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a TypeScript EventHubProducerClient that sends temperature sensor data to Azure Event Hubs using environment variables for configuration.

promptTemplates.title: Event Consumer with Logging
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement an EventHubConsumerClient that subscribes to events and logs each received event with partition ID and timestamp.

promptTemplates.title: Production Consumer with Checkpointing
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an event consumer that uses BlobCheckpointStore for checkpointing after processing each batch, with error handling for transient failures.

promptTemplates.title: Partition-Aware Event Processing
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a solution that sends events with partition keys for device-specific ordering and consumes from specific partitions starting from a given timestamp.

bestPractices: Always checkpoint after successful event processing to ensure exactly-once semantics
Use partition keys to maintain ordering for related events from the same source
Close EventHubProducerClient and EventHubConsumerClient when done to release resources
antiPatterns: Skipping checkpointing in production environments causes duplicate processing on restart
Not using partition keys when event ordering matters for business logic
Leaving producer or consumer clients open leads to resource leaks and connection exhaustion

faq.question: What is the difference between partition ID and partition key?
faq.answer: Partition ID targets a specific partition directly. Partition key uses consistent hashing to route related events to the same partition for ordering guarantees.

faq.question: When should I use checkpointing?
faq.answer: Use checkpointing in production to track processed events. It enables exactly-once processing and allows consumers to resume from the last successful position after restarts.

faq.question: How do I handle message processing failures?
faq.answer: Do not checkpoint on processing failures. The events will be reprocessed on the next consumer start. Log the error and optionally implement retry logic.

faq.question: What authentication methods are supported?
faq.answer: The skill uses DefaultAzureCredential which supports managed identity, environment credentials, Azure CLI, and Visual Studio credentials for flexible authentication.

faq.question: How do I read events from a specific point in time?
faq.answer: Use the startPosition option with enqueuedOn set to a Date object, or use offset-based positioning with specific offset values or @earliest/@latest.

faq.question: What is the purpose of consumer groups?
faq.answer: Consumer groups allow multiple independent applications to read the same event stream at their own pace. Each consumer group maintains its own checkpoint position.
